PixiJS V5 Tutorial (20): Pop a Damage Number When You Hit the Wall

In the last post we got collision detection working. This time we'll make it actually feel like something happened — when the sprite smacks into the wall, we pop a star burst and a damage number, then fade them out smoothly with alpha so the screen doesn't get cluttered.

Next up is boundaries. In pretty much every game you play there's some kind of boundary, so let's talk about how to handle them. PixiJS doesn't have a built-in boundary method, so in this post we borrow a handy `contain` helper and wire it into the bouncing sprite example from earlier.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (18) — How boxesIntersect Actually Decides a Collision

Time to circle back to something I glossed over earlier — the parameters inside boxesIntersect. We'll break down what getBounds() returns, then walk through each comparison in that long return statement step by step so you can see exactly when two boxes count as colliding and when they don't.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (17) - Adding Text to Your HP Bar

Last time we built an HP bar, but what about the text on it? In this post we cover the easy part — adding text on top of the bar with PIXI.Text, swapping in a variable for the current HP, and using the game loop (ticker) to drain health and update the number in real time.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (16) - Building a Game HP Bar

Starting from the last post, our project finally started to feel a bit like an actual game. So in this one we'll build something every game needs — an HP bar. We'll draw it ourselves with two stacked rounded rectangles and a controllable width property to represent the health percentage.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (15) - Collision Detection with Bump

Last time we learned how to draw geometric shapes in PIXI. Now it's time for something I find pretty interesting — collision detection. If you've ever Googled "PIXI hitTestRectangle" and hit a wall, this post explains why, and shows you a couple of ready-to-use functions you can drop straight into your project.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (14) - Graphics (2): Circles, Polygons, Lines and Stars

Last time we covered basic geometry drawing in PixiJS, but only the rectangle. PIXI actually gives you a bunch of other shapes to draw too — circles, ellipses, rounded rectangles, polygons, lines and even stars. So this post walks through all the other geometry methods one by one.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (13) - Graphics (1): Drawing Shapes

Time to draw some geometry with PIXI. In this post we'll go through drawing shapes with PixiJS Graphics — outlines, rectangles, fills — and then tie it back to the interaction tricks from earlier chapters so a shape becomes something you can actually click.

Let's keep going with mouse interaction in PixiJS. This time we look at the other mouse events PIXI gives you — like click — beyond just dragging an image around. You'll see how to turn on interactivity, make the cursor turn into a pointer, and do something fun on each click.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (11) - Making a Sprite Follow Your Mouse

Last time we moved a sprite around with the keyboard. This time we're doing it with the mouse. I'll show you how to use PixiJS's built-in interaction mouse coordinates, why the sprite vanishes if you forget to keep updating, and how to do the same thing with plain JavaScript event listeners if you'd rather go native.
